meaning: n.[希神][罗神]居于山林水泽的仙女,美丽的少女
examples: He also owns a 105 ft, high-tech catamaran called the Necker Nymph.
Doing that will also allow you, for a further $25, 000, to take charge of Necker Nymph, the three-seater submarine for the week.
After lunch there will be a walk in the grounds of the chateau to see the Jugendstil nymph fountains, a work dating from 1903 by Walter Schott.
Thankfully, there were no articles about double-hauling a cast or tying a prince nymph.
The Nymph is lightweight and with an open cockpit, represents a breakthrough in scuba diving technology.
